payload.text:
🚍 CollegeBus Tracker – Live College Bus Tracking & Commute App

Are you tired of standing at the bus stop wondering where your college bus is?
Do you wish you could track your ride just like you track a cab or bike using popular apps like Rapido?

If yes, CollegeBus Tracker is the app you’ve been waiting for.

Inspired by the style and user experience of commuter apps such as Rapido (see reference: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.rapido.passenger&pcampaignid=web_share
), CollegeBus Tracker brings real-time bus tracking, route maps, live ETAs, smart notifications, and safety features to students, parents, and college transport administrators — all in one place.

CollegeBus Tracker makes daily college commutes predictable, safe, and stress-free — just like you expect from top transportation apps.

📌 What Is CollegeBus Tracker?

CollegeBus Tracker is a GPS-enabled mobile app that lets you:

✔ Track your college buses in real time
✔ Know exactly when your bus will arrive
✔ View routes, stops, and schedules
✔ Receive alerts about delays or changes
✔ Contact drivers or transport staff
✔ Use safety tools such as emergency alerts

All of this — with an easy-to-use, student-friendly interface reminiscent of successful modern commute apps like the one at this link:
👉 https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.rapido.passenger&pcampaignid=web_share

But unlike ride-hailing apps, CollegeBus Tracker is built specifically for college transport — with features tailored to students, parents, and institutions.

🌟 Why You’ll Love CollegeBus Tracker

Every day, thousands of students miss buses, wait in uncertainty, or deal with communication gaps about transport status. CollegeBus Tracker changes all of that.

✔ Real-Time GPS Tracking

Just like tracking a ride in popular passenger apps, you can see the exact location of your college bus on the map, updated in real time.

No guessing. No anxiety. Just clear visibility of where your bus is and how fast it’s moving.

✔ Accurate Arrival Time (ETA) Notifications

Know precisely when your bus will reach your stop with dynamic ETA predictions that adjust as the bus moves.

Receive push notifications when:
📍 The bus is nearby
📍 The bus is delayed
📍 The bus has left the previous stop

✔ Route Map & Stop Details

Get a clear visual map view of your entire bus route — including all stops, the direction of travel, and your closest pickup point.

Whether you’re a first-year student or a returning commuter, route clarity helps you plan your day better.

✔ Alerts & Smart Notifications

Stay informed with intelligent notifications:

🔔 Bus arrival alerts
🔔 Delay alerts
🔔 Route changes
🔔 Schedule updates
🔔 Emergency messages (SOS)

Custom settings let you choose what notifications matter most to you.

✔ Student & Parent Accounts

CollegeBus Tracker supports multiple user profiles:

👩‍🎓 Student – Track your own bus, view ETAs, get alerts
👨‍👩‍👧 Parent – Monitor your child’s bus status, notifications & safety
🧑‍💼 Transport Admin – Manage routes, buses, drivers, and notifications

Everyone gets the information they need — safely and securely.

✔ Bus Schedules & Timetables

Access complete schedules for all routes:

🕐 Morning / Evening timings
📅 Weekday & weekend schedules
📝 Exam-day and special-event schedules
📍 Stop-wise timing details

Planning your day has never been this simple!

✔ Driver & Contact Info

Need to get in touch with the bus driver or transport supervisor?
CollegeBus Tracker provides direct contact numbers for:

📞 Drivers
📞 Wardens
📞 Transport office

No more guessing or searching through multiple apps to find contact information.

✔ Safety First — SOS & Alerts

Safety is our priority. In case of any emergency:

🚨 Use the SOS button to alert campus authorities immediately
📍 Your live location is shared with college officials
📱 Parents receive instant notifications

Peace of mind for students and parents alike.

✔ Easy & Secure Login

Sign in using:

📧 College email
📱 Phone number with OTP
🔒 Secure, verified access

Only authenticated users can access bus tracking and notifications.

✔ Multi-Bus & Multi-Route Support

Whether your college has a single bus or an entire fleet:

🚌 Multiple buses
🧭 Multiple routes
📊 Route-wise analytics
📍 Student route assignments

Scales with your institution’s transport needs.

🧠 How CollegeBus Tracker Works

CollegeBus Tracker simplifies your commute in five easy steps:

Login securely using your college email or registered phone number

Select your bus route and stop

Check the live GPS map to see your bus location

Get ETA and notification alerts in real time

Travel safely and confidently every day

Using this app feels familiar if you’ve ever tracked a ride in apps like Rapido (reference: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.rapido.passenger&pcampaignid=web_share
), but it’s optimized for fixed-route college transport.

📊 Benefits for All Users
For Students:

✔ No more waiting without knowing
✔ Real-time bus visibility
✔ Instant alerts for delays or changes
✔ Better planning of daily routine

For Parents:

✔ Peace of mind with live tracking
✔ Safety alerts and emergency notifications
✔ Know exactly when your child’s bus arrives
✔ Easy contact with transport staff

For Transport Admins:

✔ Real-time oversight of entire fleet
✔ Better communication with students and parents
✔ Route management and planning
✔ Efficient, data-driven transport operations

🧩 Compare With Other Transportation Apps

The reference link (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.rapido.passenger&pcampaignid=web_share
) shows how a modern commute app should feel — simple, fast, and reliable.

CollegeBus Tracker brings the same quality of user experience to college transport but adds:

🎓 Route-specific features
🎓 Scheduled pickups & fixed stops
🎓 Student & parent profiles
🎓 Safety tools designed for younger commuters
🎓 Transport admin controls

This isn’t just a tracking app — it’s a complete commuting solution for educational institutions.

🖼 Example Use Cases
🧑‍🎓 Student Experience

Your bus is scheduled at 7:30 AM.
Instead of going to the stop at 7:00 AM and waiting, you open CollegeBus Tracker and see the bus is still 10 minutes away.
You leave at just the right time and reach the stop without stress.

Notification: Bus arriving at your stop in 2 minutes.

Perfect timing every day!

👨‍👩‍👦 Parent Experience

You’re worried about your child’s commute during monsoon season.
CollegeBus Tracker notifies you:

📍 Bus is behind schedule due to traffic
📍 New ETA pushed to your phone
📍 You can see the live location too

You don’t have to call your child — and they stay safe.

🧑‍💼 Transport Admin Experience

With dozens of buses on multiple routes, managing transport is tough.
CollegeBus Tracker helps you:

📊 Monitor all buses in real time
🗺 Map route progress
🔔 Send alerts to students about delays
📄 Export reports for planning

College transport just got easier.

🆕 Future Upgrades (Planned)

We are constantly improving CollegeBus Tracker with features such as:

💡 AI-powered ETA predictions
📍 Stop-based attendance check-in
📢 In-app announcements
📊 Analytics dashboards for admin
🌐 Multi-college support

Your feedback drives our roadmap!

🏆 What Users Are Saying

⭐ “Finally, a bus app that works for college travel!” — Student
⭐ “I love the real-time tracking and alerts.” — Parent
⭐ “Transport management is streamlined and efficient.” — Admin
⭐ “Feels as easy as tracking a Rapido ride!” — User

(Yes, just like the experience shown here: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.rapido.passenger&pcampaignid=web_share
)

📲 Download CollegeBus Tracker Today

Stop guessing.
Stop waiting.
Start tracking.

Get CollegeBus Tracker now and make your daily commute predictable, safe, and stress-free — just like modern ride apps should be.

👉 Install now and experience real-time college bus tracking!

📌 Permissions & Privacy

📎 We only request necessary permissions to:
✔ Access location for tracking
✔ Send notifications
✔ Save account details securely

Your data is protected and used only for improving your commute experience.

📫 Need Support?

If you have questions, feedback, or feature requests:

📧 Support Email: support@collegebustracker.com

📞 Contact: +91-XXXXXXXXXX

We’d love to hear from you!

**Initiating the Concept**

I'm now zeroing in on the core concept: a CollegeBus Tracker app. My focus is on emulating the clean, user-friendly interface of ride-hailing apps like Rapido, but tailored to track college buses. The aim is to create a map-centric experience for real-time bus locations and commute information.


**Expanding Feature Scope**

I'm now integrating an AI-powered chatbot and image analysis, both leveraging Gemini. My architecture incorporates a `metadata.json` file for app info, while the core will leverage React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. The design approach is mobile-first, responsive, and emulates the user experience of apps like Rapido. Recharts will be used for dashboard analytics.


**Defining the App Structure**

I'm solidifying the app's structure now. I'm focusing on the component breakdown, with files for the entry point, the main router/layout, the types, and Gemini services, all clearly defined. I'm prioritizing simplicity with a state-based view manager or HashRouter, similar to Rapido but tailored to my use case. Key components are being fleshed out like the map interface, chatbot, image analyzer, and admin dashboard.


**Refining Visual Design**

I'm now incorporating a modern color palette: yellow-400 as primary and slate-900 as secondary, aiming for that "Rapido-inspired" feel. I am also working on the `geminiService.ts` file, where I have defined the chat and image analysis functions. I realize I need to use `ai.models.generateContent` for both functions, as the instructions provided have stated.

I'm developing a simulated map using SVG or CSS animations within `MapInterface.tsx`, as I can't embed a real Google Map without an API key. I'll also add a bottom sheet showing trip details. The standard chat UI in `AIChatbot.tsx` will include a message list and input field, with a system instruction. Finally, I'll be working on the image analyzer within `ImageAnalyzer.tsx`.


**Enhancing Visuals & Functionality**

I'm now focusing on a file input for image analysis and the core `App.tsx` state management to handle different views. I am also working on the Gemini Service implementation, using a relative container, animating an SVG "Bus" icon on a simulated map route for visualization, adding rounded corners, shadows, and prominent buttons. Furthermore, metadata is getting an update to include camera and geolocation, and type definitions for Bus and Stop are getting defined.


**Refining Component Interactions**

I'm now refining interactions between components. The Gemini Service logic is split to handle image and text prompts differently. I'm focusing on elegant loading states within the AI Chatbot to improve the user experience. Also, the ImageAnalyzer component is being updated to properly convert images to base64 format for Gemini input.

**Finalizing Implementation Steps**

I'm taking note of all requirements now. The AI Chatbot is being implemented using gemini-3-pro-preview. Also, I am allowing users to upload a photo for image analysis using gemini-3-pro-preview. React 18 is being utilized via `createRoot` and Tailwind is in use via the `cdn` script.

**Integrating App Components**

I'm now consolidating the app's overall implementation by focusing on D3/Recharts for the Admin view. A Recharts graph will satisfy the route efficiency requirement. I'm also creating a custom SVG map to avoid external API dependencies. Lastly, I'm ensuring images are read as base64 and stripped of the header before sending to Gemini, sending to `inlineData`.


**Finalizing Key Implementation**

I'm now focusing on ensuring that `process.env.API_KEY` is utilized correctly, and that `gemini-3-pro-preview` is used for both text and image functionalities as specified. I'm also confirming the design fully aligns with the specified Rapido aesthetic.

I'm now focusing on accessibility and ensuring that the contrast and responsive classes are present, and that error handling is considered for all the AI functionalities.
\n\n
**Implementing Core Components**

I'm now implementing the core components. I'm focusing on `MapInterface` that will show "Live" location, simulating a bus moving between stops. The `BottomNav` is being designed with icons for Map, Chat, Scan, and Admin. Lastly, I'm working on the `AdminDashboard` using Recharts to display "On-time Performance". I am also starting to write the code for these components.

entries.description: Root HTML file with Tailwind CSS.
Service for interacting with Gemini API.

import React from 'react';

export const BotIcon = ({ className }: { className?: string }) => (
  <svg 
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" 
    viewBox="0 0 24 24" 
    fill="none" 
    stroke="currentColor" 
    strokeWidth="2" 
    strokeLinecap="round" 
    strokeLinejoin="round" 
    className={className}
  >
    <path d="M12 8V4H8" />
    <rect width="16" height="12" x="4" y="8" rx="2" />
    <path d="M2 14h2" />
    <path d="M20 14h2" />
    <path d="M15 13v2" />
    <path d="M9 13v2" />
  </svg>
);
